basic_details.job_title: JSSH Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 25 Faculty and Specialist Gr III Posts
basic_details.short_information: Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital (JSSH) Faculty and Specialist Gr III Notification 2026 has been released through Advertisement No. F.9(280)/JSSH/Estt./Recruitment/2026 on the official website www.jsshs.org. A total of 25 vacancies have been announced through the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.age_details: Professor: 60 years, Associate Professor: 50 years, Assistant Professor: 45 years, Specialist Gr. III: 45 years
age_limit.details: Professor: Maximum age limit is 60 years. Associate Professor: Maximum age limit is 50 years. Assistant Professor: Maximum age limit is 45 years. Specialist Gr. III: Maximum age limit is 45 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Medical qualifications as per recruitment rules, with specific post-graduate degrees and experience requirements.
qualification.details: Professor Cardiology: A medical qualification included in Schedule I or II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification in Cardiology or recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Four years experience as Associate Professor or Six years as Assistant Professor. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Professor Anaesthesia: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Anaesthesia or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Nine years teaching experience from a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Anaesthesia or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Professor Gastroenterology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. MD Medicine/Paediatric or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto in the respective discipline or subject; D.M. in Medical Gastroenterology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Four years experience as Associate Professor or Six years as Assistant Professor. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Professor Nephrology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. MD Medicine /Paediatric or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto in the respective discipline or subject. D.M. in Nephrology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Four years experience as Associate Professor or Six years as Assistant Professor. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Professor Neurology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. MD Medicine/Paediatric or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto in the respective discipline or subject. D.M. in Neurology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Four years experience as Associate Professor or Six years as Assistant Professor. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Professor Radiology: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Nine years teaching experience from a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Eight Publications in National (4) and International Journal (4). Associate Professor Gastroenterology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. MD or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto in the respective discipline or subject. DM in the Medical Gastroenterology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Two (02) years experience as Assistant Professor. Four Publications in National (2) and International Journal (2) mandatory. Associate Professor Nephrology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification e.g. M.D or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. DM in Nephrology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Two (02) years experience as Assistant Professor. Four Publications in National (2) and International Journal (2) mandatory. Associate Professor Neurology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification e.g. M.D or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. DM in the Neurology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Two (02) years experience as Assistant Professor. Four Publications in National (2) and International Journal (2) mandatory. Associate Professor Radiology: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Five years teaching experience from a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Four Publications in National (2) and International Journal (2). Assistant Professor Anaesthesia: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Anaesthesia or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Three (03) years teaching experience from a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Anaesthesia or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Assistant Professor Gastroenterology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification e.g. M.D or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. DM in the Medical Gastroenterology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Assistant Professor Nephrology: A Medical qualification in schedule I or II or part II of the third schedule to the Indian Medical council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification e.g. M.D or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. DM in Nephrology or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Assistant Professor Radiology: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Three (03) years teaching experience from a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Specialist Gr. III Cardiology: A medical qualification included in schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, postgraduate qualification, i.e. MD in Medicine or Pediatrics or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. D. M. Cardiology/ or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Specialist Gr. III CTVS: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, MS in General Surgery. A postgraduate qualification in concerned Speciality i.e. MCh (CTVS), D.N.B (CTVS) or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Specialist Gr. III Neurology: A medical qualification included in schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, A postgraduate qualification, i.e. MD in Medicine or Pediatrics or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. D. M. Neurology/ or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Specialist Gr. III Neurosurgery: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, MS in General Surgery. A postgraduate qualification in concerned Speciality i.e. MCh (Neurosurgery), D.N.B (Neurosurgery) or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Specialist Gr. III Nuclear Medicine: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, A postgraduate qualification in concerned Speciality i.e. M.N.A.M.S (Nuclear Medicine) or DNB (Nuclear Medicine) or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Three year experience in the concerned speciality after obtaining the post Graduation. Specialist Gr. III Oncology: A recognized MBBS qualification included in the First or Second Schedule or Part II of the Third Schedule (other than licentiate qualification in the Indian Medical Council Act. 1956 Holders of Education qualifications included in Part II of the Third Schedule should also fulfill the conditions stipulated in sub-section (3) of section(13) of the Indian Medical Council Act 1956. A post graduate degree in the concerned speciality (MD/DNB) Doctorate of Medicine (Medical Oncology) or Diplomate National Board (Medical Oncology). Specialist Gr. III Radiology: A medical qualification included in Schedule I & II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, A postgraduate qualification i.e. M.D in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a recognized qualification Education Qualification equivalent thereto. Three years senior residency experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Radiology or Education Qualification equivalent foreign/Indian degree or a qualification recognized Education Qualification equivalent thereto.

salary_details.pay_scale: Professor: Rs. 3,05,000/- per month, Associate Professor: Rs. 2,55,000/- per month, Assistant Professor: Rs. 1,95,000/- per month, Specialist Gr. III: As per revised pay rules Level 11
salary_details.professor: Rs. 3,05,000/- per month (Inclusive of all allowances)
salary_details.associate professor: Rs. 2,55,000/- per month (Inclusive of all allowances)
salary_details.assistant professor: Rs. 1,95,000/- per month (Inclusive of all allowances)
salary_details.specialist grade - iii: As per revised pay rules Level 11 (Pay Rs. 67,700/- plus admissible allowances per month)

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for JSSH Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: www.jsshs.org.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 17-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts is 17th June 2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well in advance to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 25 vacancies announced for the Faculty and Specialist Gr III positions at Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital. These vacancies are distributed across various specializations as detailed in the official notification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III recruitment?
faq.answer: The application mode for the Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital Faculty and Specialist Gr III recruitment is strictly offline. Candidates need to download the application form and send it via post to the specified address.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for these posts?
faq.answer: The educational qualification varies by post but generally includes medical qualifications as per recruitment rules, with specific post-graduate degrees and relevant experience required for each specialization. Please refer to the official notification for detailed qualification criteria.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for applying to the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts?
faq.answer: The age limit varies for different posts: Professor (up to 60 years), Associate Professor (up to 50 years), Assistant Professor (up to 45 years), and Specialist Gr. III (up to 45 years). Age relaxation rules may apply as per government norms.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary offered for the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III positions?
faq.answer: The salary structure is different for each cadre. Professors can expect around Rs. 3,05,000/- per month, Associate Professors around Rs. 2,55,000/- per month, and Assistant Professors around Rs. 1,95,000/- per month. Specialist Gr. III will receive salary as per revised pay rules Level 11.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts at Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital will primarily involve an interview. Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for the interview stage.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I download the application form for JSSH recruitment?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official website of Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital, which is www.jsshs.org. It is advisable to download the form from the official source to ensure accuracy.

faq.question: Q9. What documents are required to be submitted with the application form?
faq.answer: Candidates are required to attach self-attested copies of necessary documents, which typically include educational certificates, experience certificates, proof of identity, and a recent passport-sized photograph. Refer to the official notification for a comprehensive list.

faq.question: Q10. Is there any application fee for JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts?
faq.answer: Yes, there is an application fee. Unreserved category candidates need to pay Rs. 1000/-, while OBC/SC/ST candidates are required to pay Rs. 500/-. The fee details are mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for the JSSH Faculty and Specialist Gr III recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the Janakpuri Super Speciality Hospital Faculty and Specialist Gr III posts is 17th June 2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well in advance to avoid any last-minute issues.
